features.description: Initialize project with package.json, tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts, tsup config, and all dependencies (typescript, vitest, tsup)
steps: Given an empty output/ directory
When the project is initialized with package.json (name: mini-jwt, type: module), tsconfig.json (strict mode, ESM), vitest.config.ts, and tsup.config.ts
Then `npm install` succeeds, `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0, and the directory structure matches the spec

features.description: Define all core TypeScript types: JWTHeader, JWTPayload, VerifyResult, and KeyPair in src/types.ts
steps: Given src/types.ts is created
When a TypeScript file imports JWTHeader, JWTPayload, VerifyResult, and KeyPair from './types'
Then the compiler accepts assignments matching each interface shape without errors

features.description: Implement base64UrlEncode(data: Uint8Array): string in src/base64url.ts
steps: Given a Uint8Array containing UTF-8 bytes
When base64UrlEncode is called with that array
Then it returns a string using base64url alphabet (no +, /, or = padding characters)

features.description: Implement base64UrlDecode(str: string): Uint8Array in src/base64url.ts
steps: Given a valid base64url-encoded string (with or without padding)
When base64UrlDecode is called with that string
Then it returns a Uint8Array whose contents exactly match the original pre-encoded bytes

features.description: base64UrlEncode and base64UrlDecode are inverses (round-trip property)
steps: Given arbitrary byte data encoded with base64UrlEncode
When that encoded string is passed to base64UrlDecode
Then the output Uint8Array is byte-for-byte identical to the original input

features.description: Implement generateKeyPair(): Promise<KeyPair> in src/keys.ts using Web Crypto API with ECDSA P-256
steps: Given the Web Crypto API is available
When generateKeyPair() is called
Then it resolves with an object containing a CryptoKey publicKey and a CryptoKey privateKey, both with algorithm.name === 'ECDSA' and namedCurve === 'P-256'

features.description: Generated key pair has correct key usages: privateKey allows 'sign', publicKey allows 'verify'
steps: Given a key pair generated by generateKeyPair()
When the key usages are inspected
Then privateKey.usages includes 'sign' and publicKey.usages includes 'verify'

features.description: Implement sign(payload: JWTPayload, privateKey: CryptoKey): Promise<string> in src/sign.ts
steps: Given a valid JWTPayload and an ES256 private key
When sign() is called
Then it resolves with a string containing exactly two '.' characters, producing three base64url-encoded segments

features.description: sign() produces a header segment with alg: 'ES256' and typ: 'JWT'
steps: Given a signed JWT returned by sign()
When the first segment is base64url-decoded and JSON-parsed
Then the resulting object has alg === 'ES256' and typ === 'JWT'

features.description: sign() auto-sets iat to current Unix timestamp when not provided in payload
steps: Given a JWTPayload with no iat field
When sign() is called
Then the decoded payload contains an iat value approximately equal to Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)

features.description: sign() preserves caller-supplied iat when already present in payload
steps: Given a JWTPayload with iat explicitly set to a specific Unix timestamp
When sign() is called
Then the decoded payload's iat equals the original caller-supplied value unchanged

features.description: sign() encodes the full payload including all standard and custom claims
steps: Given a JWTPayload with fields: sub, iss, exp, and a custom 'role' claim
When sign() is called and the second segment is decoded
Then the parsed object contains all supplied claims with their original values

features.description: sign() with an empty payload object produces a valid JWT with only iat injected
steps: Given an empty JWTPayload {}
When sign() is called
Then it resolves with a three-segment JWT whose decoded payload contains only the auto-injected iat

features.description: Implement verify(token, publicKey, options?): Promise<VerifyResult> in src/verify.ts — valid token returns valid=true
steps: Given a JWT signed with a private key and verified with the matching public key
When verify() is called with no options
Then it resolves with { valid: true, payload: <decoded claims>, errors: [] }

features.description: verify() returns valid=false and an error when exp claim is in the past
steps: Given a JWT whose payload contains exp set to a Unix timestamp 60 seconds in the past
When verify() is called with the correct public key
Then the result has valid === false and errors contains a message indicating the token is expired

features.description: verify() returns valid=true when exp claim is in the future
steps: Given a JWT whose payload contains exp set to a Unix timestamp 3600 seconds in the future
When verify() is called with the correct public key
Then the result has valid === true and errors is an empty array

features.description: verify() returns valid=false and an error when nbf claim is in the future
steps: Given a JWT whose payload contains nbf set to a Unix timestamp 60 seconds in the future
When verify() is called with the correct public key
Then the result has valid === false and errors contains a message indicating the token is not yet valid

features.description: verify() returns valid=true when nbf claim is in the past
steps: Given a JWT whose payload contains nbf set to a Unix timestamp 60 seconds in the past
When verify() is called with the correct public key
Then the result has valid === true and errors is empty

features.description: verify() validates audience: returns valid=false when aud does not match options.audience
steps: Given a JWT with aud claim set to 'service-a' and verify() called with options.audience = 'service-b'
When verify() resolves
Then the result has valid === false and errors contains an audience mismatch message

features.description: verify() returns valid=true when aud matches options.audience (single string)
steps: Given a JWT with aud claim 'my-api' and verify() called with options.audience = 'my-api'
When verify() resolves
Then the result has valid === true and errors is empty

features.description: verify() accepts audience when aud is an array containing options.audience
steps: Given a JWT with aud claim ['my-api', 'other-service'] and verify() called with options.audience = 'my-api'
When verify() resolves
Then the result has valid === true and errors is empty

features.description: verify() skips audience check when options.audience is not provided
steps: Given a JWT with aud claim 'some-service' and verify() called with no options
When verify() resolves
Then the result has valid === true (audience is not enforced)

features.description: verify() returns valid=false when signature has been tampered (payload bytes altered)
steps: Given a valid JWT whose middle (payload) segment has been replaced with a different base64url string
When verify() is called with the original public key
Then the result has valid === false and errors contains a signature verification failure message

features.description: verify() returns valid=false when the signature segment itself is corrupted
steps: Given a valid JWT whose third (signature) segment has been replaced with random base64url bytes
When verify() is called with the original public key
Then the result has valid === false and errors contains a signature verification failure message

features.description: verify() returns valid=false when verified with a different (wrong) public key
steps: Given a JWT signed with key pair A
When verify() is called with the public key from key pair B
Then the result has valid === false due to signature mismatch

features.description: verify() accumulates multiple errors when several claims fail simultaneously
steps: Given a JWT with both an expired exp and a future nbf
When verify() is called with the correct public key
Then the result has valid === false and errors contains entries for both the expiry and not-before failures

features.description: Implement decode(token: string): JWTPayload in src/decode.ts — returns parsed payload without verification
steps: Given any valid JWT string (including expired ones)
When decode() is called
Then it returns the JWTPayload object parsed from the second segment without performing any signature check

features.description: decode() successfully decodes an expired token without error
steps: Given a JWT whose exp claim is in the past
When decode() is called
Then it returns the payload object including the expired exp value without throwing

features.description: decode() throws an error when given a token with fewer than 3 segments
steps: Given a malformed token string with only one or two dot-separated segments
When decode() is called
Then it throws an error indicating the token is malformed

features.description: decode() throws an error when given a token with more than 3 segments
steps: Given a malformed token string with four or more dot-separated segments
When decode() is called
Then it throws an error indicating the token is malformed

features.description: decode() throws when the payload segment is not valid base64url-encoded JSON
steps: Given a three-segment token whose second segment decodes to non-JSON bytes
When decode() is called
Then it throws an error indicating the payload could not be parsed

features.description: verify() handles a token string with the wrong number of segments gracefully (returns valid=false, not a throw)
steps: Given a token string with only two segments passed to verify()
When verify() is called
Then it resolves (does not reject) with valid === false and an appropriate error message

features.description: src/index.ts re-exports all public API symbols: generateKeyPair, sign, verify, decode, base64UrlEncode, base64UrlDecode, and all types
steps: Given the compiled library
When a consumer imports from 'mini-jwt'
Then generateKeyPair, sign, verify, decode, base64UrlEncode, and base64UrlDecode are all importable named exports

features.description: Write tests/sign.test.ts covering sign() normal flow, iat injection, iat preservation, and empty payload
steps: Given tests/sign.test.ts exists with Vitest test cases
When `npx vitest run tests/sign.test.ts` is executed
Then all tests in that file pass with exit code 0

features.description: Write tests/verify.test.ts covering valid token, exp, nbf, audience, and signature tamper scenarios
steps: Given tests/verify.test.ts exists with Vitest test cases
When `npx vitest run tests/verify.test.ts` is executed
Then all tests in that file pass with exit code 0

features.description: Write tests/decode.test.ts covering successful decode, expired token decode, and malformed token errors
steps: Given tests/decode.test.ts exists with Vitest test cases
When `npx vitest run tests/decode.test.ts` is executed
Then all tests in that file pass with exit code 0

features.description: Write tests/edge-cases.test.ts covering wrong key, multi-error accumulation, array audience, missing signature, and full-claim round-trip
steps: Given tests/edge-cases.test.ts exists with Vitest test cases
When `npx vitest run tests/edge-cases.test.ts` is executed
Then all tests in that file pass with exit code 0

features.description: Full test suite passes: all four test files execute without failures via `npx vitest run`
steps: Given all source files and test files are implemented
When `npx vitest run` is executed from the output/ directory
Then all test suites pass with exit code 0 and zero failing tests
